title: Authorization, Object Ownership, Properties, Functions, and Tenancy · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent service team receives a different API, load, trust, persistence, and failure constraint and must transfer authorization, object ownership, properties, functions, and tenancy into a new deny-by-default authorization decision matrix. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of deny-by-default authorization decision matrix with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.
Load or constrain the target within the authorized tenant and ownership relationship before read, write, delete, export, or nested access.
Allowlist readable and writable properties by operation and principal, reject server-owned fields, and prevent over-posting through explicit command types.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Authz Subject Action Resource
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab team handles case 5248 in a privacy-sensitive records API using conditional requests. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce tenant, action, and property authorization as resource policy; then saturate one admission budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Authz Subject Action Resource.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for define subject, action, resource, tenant, properties, relationship, context, policy version, and decision evidence for every protected operation. For Go server Commit to the mission model · Lab Authz Subject Action Resource, keep Authz Subject Action Resource connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.
